paper_title: Application of Decision Trees in Aerobiological Study

methods.description: Statistical analysis of the data was performed using decision tree algorithms to classify and evaluate the dataset, which consisted of 8 taxa over 13 years. Various decision tree algorithms were applied to assess their analytic power and classification accuracy.

steps.description: Statistical analysis of the data was performed using the Data Mining module of STATISTICA 10 software and WEKA open source tool for data mining tasks.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using STATISTICA and WEKA

steps.description: The decision tree method is usually applied to large sets of data to create a model and check its performance.
steps.searchable_summary: apply decision tree method to create and evaluate model

steps.description: The measure of the quality of each applied tree was the percentage of correctly classified observations from both the cross-validation method and reused observations from the training set as test data.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model using cross-validation and training set

steps.description: In the cross-validation method, we divided the dataset into 13 random parts in which the classes are represented in approximately the same proportions as in the full dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: divide dataset into 13 random parts for cross-validation

steps.description: Each part is held out in turn and the algorithm is trained on the 12 remaining parts. Then, the classification accuracy is calculated on the holdout set.
steps.searchable_summary: train algorithm on 12 parts and validate on holdout set

steps.description: Finally, the averaged value of the 13 accuracies is calculated.

methods.description: Pollen monitoring was performed in Lublin, Poland, using a Hirst-type volumetric trap to record pollen data from 2001-2013. The data was used to analyze atmospheric pollen season parameters.

steps.description: Pollen monitoring was performed in Lublin (eastern Poland) during 2001-2013.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen data in Lublin from 2001-2013

steps.description: Pollen data were recorded using a Hirst-type volumetric trap.
steps.searchable_summary: record pollen data using Hirst-type volumetric trap

steps.description: Daily average pollen counts were expressed as the number of pollen grains per cubic meter of air.

The following atmospheric pollen season (APS) parameters were analyzed: season start, end, duration, maximum daily pollen concentration, date of maximum pollen concentration, and Seasonal Pollen Index.
Pollen seasons were calculated using the 95% method, defining the start and end of the season based on cumulative pollen count.
